meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit to overcome the no portfolio problem. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first app.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends when you stop building toy projects and deploy a production-grade SaaS. By leveraging a SaaS starter kit with Antigravity and Claude Code, you automate the boilerplate, allowing you to focus on unique features that prove your engineering capability to future employers and clients.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank terminal, the weight of a dozen 'tutorial-hell' projects pressing down on you. You have the skills, but your GitHub profile is a graveyard of abandoned to-do lists and weather apps. The industry doesn't care about your certifications; they care about the live, breathing SaaS applications you’ve shipped to the world.

Relief is found in the architecture of modern SaaS starter kits. By abstracting the redundant plumbing—auth, billing, and database schemas—you finally have the leverage to build something substantial. This is your transition from a student writing code to an engineer building products.

steps.title: Initialize the Foundation
steps.description: Select a robust SaaS starter kit to handle authentication and database configuration. This provides an immediate professional structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Next.js boilerplate with Supabase integration and Tailwind CSS styling.

steps.title: Define Your Unique Value Proposition
steps.description: Add a specific feature that solves a real-world problem. Avoid generic CRUD apps; build a specialized tool.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a new API route that processes user input using an LLM to generate professional summaries.

steps.title: Implement Stripe Billing
steps.description: Nothing proves professional competency like handling real money. Configure Stripe to demonstrate you understand SaaS economics.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate Stripe subscription tiers into the existing user dashboard layout.

steps.title: Automate Testing and Quality
steps.description: Expert engineers never ship without tests. Automate your unit tests to ensure your app remains stable as you scale.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write Playwright integration tests for the authentication flow and checkout process.

steps.title: Deployment and Production Readiness
steps.description: Push your code to Vercel or Railway to get a live URL. A live URL is your most powerful resume asset.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment configuration file for Vercel ensuring all environment variables are mapped.

pro_tips: Treat your README as a product marketing page, not a technical manual; explain the problem you solved, not the libraries you used.
Always include a 'View Live Demo' button at the very top of your repository to reduce friction for recruiters.
Record a 60-second Loom video demonstrating the app's core value and embed it in your portfolio site.
Focus on one 'hero' feature that demonstrates complex state management rather than ten simple features.
Keep your commit history clean; it serves as a timeline of your engineering discipline and project evolution.
content.next_steps: Once your app is live, iterate on feedback by adding one 'user-requested' feature per week. Document your journey on social media to build your personal brand alongside your code.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a student; you are a builder. Stop waiting for permission to start your career and begin shipping high-impact software today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
